company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Life Science

supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on limited number of vendors for utilities (including PG&E for San Francisco Bay Area properties), security, construction services, and third-party contractors for construction materials and skilled labor
risks: Supply chain disruptions for construction materials and labor; commodity price volatility; potential PG&E service interruptions due to wildfire risks and bankruptcy history; dependence on third-party suppliers for construction projects; potential delays in obtaining materials from suppliers impacted by economic or political changes

Ground lease obligations with potential for significant up-front or increased recurring payments upon extension; construction cost increases and delays; tenant credit quality and collectibility risks; concentration of properties in specific geographic markets subject to local economic conditions

regulatory_risks: FDA regulatory approval delays or changes affecting life science tenants
Real estate zoning, permitting, and entitlement requirements
Tenant operations subject to FDA regulations, CLIA certifications, biosafety level guidelines, radioactive materials licensing
California Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act (Proposition 65)
Prohibited transaction tax risk on real estate asset sales (100% tax unless safe harbor requirements met)
Climate-related disclosure requirements (SEC proposed standards, California SB 253 and SB 261)
Zoning, land use, and building permit regulations
California Proposition 13 potential repeal or amendment affecting property taxes
State and local tax compliance across multiple jurisdictions
Tax law changes (including potential Section 1031 Exchange amendments)
NIH and government funding changes affecting research tenants
Securities regulations as publicly traded REIT

Climate change regulations including state and local laws on climate disclosures and emission reduction targets (California GHG disclosure requirements, Boston/Cambridge/New York/Seattle building emission limits)
Municipal legislation eliminating natural gas use in new construction (New York City, San Francisco)
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code
Building emissions regulations and climate change policies
Basel III banking regulations affecting financing availability
Data privacy and security laws (California Consumer Privacy Act, California Privacy Rights Act)
Foreign operations regulatory environment (Canada)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance for international operations
Environmental compliance requirements (Phase I assessments, asbestos remediation, hazardous waste handling)
Environmental laws and regulations (hazardous materials, asbestos, environmental contamination)
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) compliance requirements
Healthcare reform and drug pricing regulations (including Inflation Reduction Act of 2022)
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code (90% distribution requirement, asset and income tests)

sanction_risks: EU retaliatory tariffs affecting tenants with European operations
Russia sanctions and potential cyberattack retaliation risks
Potential tariffs on imports from China, Canada, Mexico affecting supply chain costs
Impact of US-China trade tensions and tariffs on construction materials (steel, aluminum, solar panels)
